steps.title: Identify the parallel verb structure
steps.content: The relative clause contains “which typically are woven from willow and ______ colorful beading,” where the subject “which” (baskets) connects to two verbs via “and”: first “are woven,” then the blank. For these coordinate verbs to work grammatically, they must take the same form.

steps.title: Apply the verb parallelism rule
steps.content:
Verbs joined by “and” in a compound predicate must be in the same tense.

The first verb “are woven” is present tense, so the second verb must be too. “Feature” (D) is in simple present, maintaining parallelism.

The other options are past forms—featured, had featured, and were featuring—which violate this rule.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
